headline: Attackers turn neglected software and aging hardware into instruments of theft, exploiting a Gravity SMTP plugin flaw to harvest secrets while the new AryStinger botnet conscripts thousands of outdated D-Link routers.

emerging_trends.text: Unauthenticated WordPress plugins keep bleeding the credentials that unlock everything downstream, as the Gravity SMTP flaw hands attackers API keys, OAuth tokens, and configuration data from roughly 100,000 sites.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Gravity SMTP Plugin Leaks WordPress Secrets

emerging_trends.text: Aging consumer hardware stays free real estate for criminals, with the AryStinger botnet quietly pulling more than 4,000 unpatched routers into a network that launders malicious traffic.
End-of-life D-Link routers running firmware that no longer receives fixes have become a durable proxy layer for attackers who route their traffic through someone else's hardware.

topics.title: AryStinger Botnet Hijacks D-Link Routers
topics.latest_developments: Researchers documented AryStinger for the first time, a previously unknown botnet that has compromised more than 4,000 outdated D-Link routers worldwide and turned them into proxies for malicious traffic.
topics.summary: AryStinger infects aging D-Link routers and relays attacker traffic through them, masking its origin. Owners of old D-Link hardware should patch where fixes exist or replace devices that no longer receive firmware updates.

sources.title: AryStinger botnet infected thousands of D-Link routers worldwide

topics.title: Gravity SMTP Plugin Leaks WordPress Secrets
topics.latest_developments: Threat actors began actively exploiting CVE-2026-4020, a CVSS 5.3 information-disclosure bug in the Gravity SMTP WordPress plugin, to pull configuration data, API keys, secrets, and OAuth tokens from unauthenticated requests.
topics.summary: Gravity SMTP routes outgoing mail for about 100,000 WordPress sites. The flaw lets unauthenticated attackers read sensitive configuration and credential data, so administrators should update the plugin and rotate any exposed keys and tokens at once.

sources.title: Hackers exploit info disclosure bug in Gravity SMTP WordPress plugin

weather: Tonight: Mostly Cloudy then Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, low 62F.
Monday: Chance Showers And Thunderstorms then Showers And Thunderstorms, high 77F.

games.recap: McCarthy hits leadoff inside-the-park homer off Skenes in Rockies' 2-1 win over Pirates

business_politics.title: Trump Threatens Iran Strike as Hormuz Closes Anew
business_politics.latest_developments: Trump threatened today to strike Iran over renewed Israel-Hezbollah fighting in Lebanon, and Tehran said it has again closed the Strait of Hormuz, both escalations beyond Sunday's opening of talks.
business_politics.summary: Vice President JD Vance and senior Iranian negotiators opened talks in Switzerland on Sunday, June 21, meant to lock in Trump's earlier accord; fresh Hezbollah-Israel combat and Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz, the chokepoint for roughly a fifth of seaborne oil, now jeopardize the deal.

sources.title: Trump Threatens to Strike Iran Over Hezbollah as Lebanon Snarls Talks
Iran says it closed Strait of Hormuz after Israel and Hizbollah exchange fire
US and Iran hold crunch talks in Switzerland

business_politics.title: Starmer Near Quitting After Burnham By-Election Win
business_politics.latest_developments: Andy Burnham's by-election victory pushed Keir Starmer to the brink of resigning, with some ministers expecting him to announce his exit next week.
business_politics.summary: Keir Starmer, Britain's Labour prime minister, faces resignation after Andy Burnham, the Greater Manchester mayor, won a Commons seat, positioning Burnham to become the country's seventh prime minister since the 2016 Brexit vote as gilt markets weigh the turnover.

sources.title: Starmer on brink of quitting as UK prime minister
Starmer could announce exit next week, some ministers believe

business.title: POGOH Wins Grant to Expand Bike Share
business.latest_developments: POGOH landed a $1.5 million grant to extend its bike-share network past Pittsburgh's borders.
business.summary: POGOH, Pittsburgh's nonprofit bike-share operator, won a $1.5 million grant to add stations in communities beyond the city limits, widening the system's reach into surrounding municipalities.

sources.title: POGOH’s next ride: $1.5M grant to fuel expansion of bike share program beyond city limits

business.title: Post-Gazette Revives Dateline Pittsburgh
business.latest_developments: The Pittsburgh Post-Gazette announced it will bring back Dateline Pittsburgh and explained how local companies can take part.
business.summary: The Pittsburgh Post-Gazette is reviving Dateline Pittsburgh, a feature that publishes news and announcements from area companies, and laid out how local businesses can submit theirs.

sources.title: Dateline Pittsburgh is returning. Here's how local companies can participate

around_town.title: County Paid-Leave Plan May Sideline Teachers
around_town.latest_developments: Allegheny County's proposed paid parental leave benefit could keep teachers out for up to half a school year, prompting districts to question how they would staff the gaps.
around_town.summary: Allegheny County's proposed paid parental leave policy could let teachers take leave covering as much as half a school year, and the Post-Gazette reports school districts wonder how they would cover such long absences.

sources.title: Allegheny County’s paid parental leave proposal could have teachers out for half the school year. Can districts accommodate?

around_town.title: New Kensington Residents Form Advocacy Group
around_town.latest_developments: Jurgen Koster formed a New Kensington community group to tackle local problems and will host a cookout Tuesday, June 23.
around_town.summary: Jurgen Koster, a three-year New Kensington resident frustrated that little has changed since he arrived, started a community advocacy group that holds a cookout Tuesday, June 23, to draw neighbors in.

sources.title: New Kensington community group forms to tackle local issues, hosting cookout Tuesday

events.title: South Side Street Fest Opens Strong
events.latest_developments: The South Side Street Fest opened Saturday, June 20, drawing largely positive reviews on a calmer-than-usual weekend along East Carson Street.
events.summary: The South Side Hospitality Partnership's new South Side Street Fest, which closes East Carson Street on the South Side, opened Saturday, June 20, with attendees telling KDKA the event felt safe and welcoming.

sources.title: South Side Street Fest opens to largely positive reviews

events.title: Carnegie Museum Plans 'Egypt on the Nile'
events.latest_developments: The Carnegie Museum of Natural History announced 'Egypt on the Nile,' an exhibition opening May 1, 2027.
events.summary: The Carnegie Museum of Natural History in Oakland will open 'Egypt on the Nile' on May 1, 2027, an exhibition tracing how ancient Egyptians lived along the Nile River and the landscapes around it.

sources.title: Carnegie Museum of Natural History to open ‘Egypt on the Nile’ exhibition in 2027

around_teams.title: Pirates Weigh Catching Plan After Bart Trade
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette mailbag laid out the Pirates' catching options after the Joey Bart trade and weighed whether Marcell Ozuna returns.
around_teams.summary: Having dealt catcher Joey Bart to the Braves for reliever Hunter Stratton, the Pirates now lean on Henry Davis behind the plate, the Post-Gazette mailbag explained, and addressed whether designated hitter Marcell Ozuna comes back.

sources.title: Pirates mailbag: What's the plan at catcher after the Joey Bart trade? Is Marcell Ozuna back?

around_teams.title: Spears-Jennings's 40 Time Lifted His Draft Stock
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette detailed how rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings's 4.32-second 40-yard dash reshaped his draft trajectory.
around_teams.summary: Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings ran the 40-yard dash in 4.32 seconds at the NFL combine, a time the Post-Gazette reports vaulted his draft stock and changed how teams saw him.

sources.title: How Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings changed his NFL trajectory in 4.32 seconds

team_usa.title: USMNT Reaches Round of 32
team_usa.latest_developments: After topping Group D, the United States men's team advances to the round of 32, and ESPN makes the case this squad feels genuinely different.
team_usa.summary: The United States men's national team, co-host of the 2026 World Cup, won Group D and reached the round of 32, with ESPN arguing the team's belief and chemistry give supporters real reason to dream.

sources.title: If there was a time to dream big about the USMNT

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 2)
reading.summary: Ed Zitron extends his case that Silicon Valley sits in an unsustainable bubble, building on his scoop that OpenAI spent $34 billion in 2025 to generate $13.07 billion in revenue.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Michael Morton About E-Commerce in the Age of AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Michael Morton on how AI reshapes e-commerce, covering unfalsifiable bear cases, distribution versus referral models, grocery, and autonomous vehicles.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: AI Isn’t Breaking Work. It’s Already Broken.
reading.summary: Cal Newport argues that knowledge work broke long before AI arrived, and AI merely exposes the dysfunction already built into how people work.
